rulings.Case Title: PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. WHITE SNOW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton Andmoiton To Compel Martin Eng To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanction Of $1772.50;
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Martin Eng To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanction Of $1772.50. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The unopposed motion is granted. Defendant Martin Eng shall serve further verified responses to Special Interrogatory Nos. 4, 5, 7-14, 16, 17, 20, 22, 23, and 27 by no later than June 13, 2014. Defendant Eng's responses were evasive, incomplete, and/or non-responsive. All objections are waived. Defendant Eng shall pay plaintiff sanctions of $650 no later than July 3, 2014. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JORDAN ROSENBERG VS. MONROE VILLAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Of Specially Appearing Springpoint Deft'S Mtn To Quash Summons And Dismiss First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 1: Specially Appearing Springpoint Defendants SPRINGPOINT AT MONROE, INC. (FORMERLY KNOWN AS THE PRESBYTERIAN HOME AT MONROE, INC.), TED ALTER, SUSAN WOOD, and REBECCA FELLER'S Motion To Quash Summons And Dismiss First Amended Complaint. The motion to quash is granted. "When a nonresident defendant challenges personal jurisdiction, the plaintiff must prove, by a preponderance of the evidence, the factual basis that would justify the exercise of jurisdiction." Dorel Industries, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1267, 1273. Plaintiff fails to show that defendants (Springpoint at Monroe, Inc. Ted Alter, Susan Wood, and Rebecca Feller) had any contacts with California to warrant the exercise of general or specific jurisdiction over them. The court denies plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery. In addition, the case must be dismissed because plaintiff failed to serve defendants in a timely fashion. See CCP 583.210 and 583.250. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: PAULINE HAMILTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 5, PAULINE HAMILTON's Petition For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Non-party Hamilton's petition is granted in part and denied in part. The petition is governed by California law. CCP 2029.500. Ms. Hamilton shall sit for a deposition, but the deposition shall not require Ms. Hamilton (1) to disclose marital communications subject to Evidence Code § 980; (2) to answer questions solely about her own personal financial condition; or (3) to answer direct questions relating to Ms. Hamilton's minor son or child support payments for the minor's benefit. These limitations will protect the best interests of the minor son and to protect all applicable rights to privacy. The purpose of the deposition is to inquire into Mr. Lindseth's financial condition, and not the financial condition of Ms. Hamilton. To the extent that the deposition's scope is limited in this manner, it does not appear to implicate Ms. Hamilton's right to privacy. Other than stated in this order, Ms. Hamilton's petition is denied. Any applicable objections or assertions of privilege can be made during the course of the deposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TSHAITU DAVIS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Sumary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 16: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication. Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Plaintiff has conceded that Counts One and Two of the Complaint are barred by statute. As to Count Three, the sidewalk defect did not pose a substantial risk of harm. The dispute as to the height of the sidewalk defect is immaterial because several decisions have held height differentials of up to 1 ½" trivial as a matter of law and neither party argues that the defect here is greater than 1 ½". (See Stathoulis v. City of Montebello (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 559, 568 (collecting decisions in which trivial defects ranged from ¾ inch to 1 ½").) In addition, Plaintiff has not presented any evidence that establishes surrounding circumstances that amount to aggravating factors. Accordingly, as a matter of law the sidewalk defect was trivial and summary judgment is proper given the disposition of Counts One and Two. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, et al VS. HAROLD WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Amendment Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable At L&A Family Llc;
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFFs L&A WONG FAMILY PROPERTY ASSOCIATION LLC, and LEM WONG's Motion For Protective Order Re Amendment Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable At L&A Family LLC. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendant is denied. Plaintiff has not shown good cause why a deposition of the person most knowledgeable at L&A Wong Family Property Association, LLC, a plaintiff in this action, should not be required to sit for a deposition as part of Defendant's preparation for trial. Plaintiff's assertion that Defendant Harold Wong is the PMK for some of the categories of the deposition notice is without merit. The deposition notice is only directed at the LLC's current "officers, directors, managing agents, or employees," not those formerly in any of those positions. Maldonado v. Sup. Ct., 94 Cal.App.4th 1390, 1398. The LLC should produce one of its current officers, directors, agents, or employees who have the most knowledge about each of the categories in the deposition notice and ensure that this person has all the access needed to information and documents reasonably available within the LLC. Defendant shall make reasonable allowances both in terms of duration and spacing to take into account Mr. Wong's health needs. The deposition(s) will be scheduled at a mutually agreeable time(s), but in no event shall they conclude later than June 13, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn Compelling Deft Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 8: PLAINTIFF RAY SIOTTO'S Motion Compelling Defendant Rock And Rose Landscaping To Produce Documents And For Monetary Sanctions. Granted. The motion is timely. For RFP no. 10, based on the parties' agreement, the scope of the document production shall be limited to a three year period from April 30, 2010 to April 30, 2013. Documents reflecting "complaints" by other clients of the Defendant are relevant to the issues raised in the lawsuit. "Complaint" is construed in its ordinary meaning: a statement that one is unhappy or not satisfied with something. "Complaints" shall mean any complaint regarding Defendant's work or work of improvement from Defendant's other clients that are similar to those alleged in the Complaint. (See Complaint, 8, 13.) Change orders and punch lists are excluded from this definition. "Manager" or "Managers" is defined as the corporate owners of the defendant, landscape designers, and any other recipients of client communications who supervised the work and efforts of Defendant's employees. Monetary sanctions of $1,250.00 are awarded in favor of Plaintiff and against Defendant to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. While both sides participated in an extensive meet and confer process, the position taken by Defendant limiting the definition of "complaints" to "formalized judicial" and "non-judicial complaints" was not reasonable. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC. VS. WILLIAM WONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 11: PLAINTIFF TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC.'S Motion For Prelimary Injunction. Denied. The Court must evaluate two factors in determining whether to issue a preliminary injunction: "(1) the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial and (2) the interim harm that the plaintiff would be likely to sustain if the injunction were denied as compared to the harm the defendant would be likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued." (Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729, 749.) The burden of proof is on the plaintiff. (O'Connell v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 1452, 1481.) Plaintiff has not met its evidentiary burden of establishing a likelihood of success on the merits. The Declaration of Louis Lam is conclusory and many of the statements lack foundation. For example, Plaintiff provides no competent evidence that the Board had the authority to disqualify William Wong as president and appoint Louis Lam. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FIROUZ MAHDAVI VS. OSMAN KHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, June 3, 2014, Line 13: CROSS DEFENDANT VINCENT WILSON'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. A cross-complaint may incorporate by reference allegations contained in the underlying complaint. (Pine Terrace Apartments, L.P. v. Windscape, LLC (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 1, 19; see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) 6:239, "Cross-complaints for indemnification usually incorporate by reference the allegations of the complaint as a 'shorthand' way of showing the existence of the claim for which indemnification is sought.") The majority of the authorities cited by Cross-Defendant do not address incorporation by reference. The one case cited by Cross-Defendant that addresses incorporation by references states that "a cross-complaint must itself be sufficient and cannot be cured by the pleadings in the original action, that is the complaint and answer, apart from permissible incorporation by reference." (Berg v. Investors Real Estate Loan Co. (1962) 207 Cal.App.2d 808, 816.) The Cross-Complaint states a cause of action, as the underlying complaint alleges that both Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendant contributed to plaintiff's injuries. The Court did not consider the declarations filed in support of and in opposition to the demurrer. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
